settings.title: MFA Community - global configuration
settings.description: Platform-wide MFA settings. Changes apply immediately (the Karaf configuration org.jahia.modules.mfa.extensions is updated and the components hot-reload).

enforcement.title: Enforced factors
enforcement.help: Users must have AT LEAST ONE of the checked factors configured and verify one of them at sign-in. A user with none configured is taken through inline enrollment on the login page once the grace window has elapsed. Nothing checked = no enforcement. A factor must also be enabled per site (each factor's administration page) to take part.
enforcement.factorHelp: Require this factor as part of the platform-wide policy.
enforcement.noFactors: No MFA factor modules are currently installed.

graceDays.label: Grace period (days)
graceDays.help: Days a user with no enforced factor configured may still sign in, counted per user from their first enforced sign-in attempt. 0 = immediate enforcement. Maximum 365.

gate.title: /cms/login gate
gate.help: Jahia's legacy /cms/login endpoint authenticates with username/password only and never consults MFA factors - while enforcement is active it is a second-factor bypass. The gate rejects it with 403 when an enforced factor is enabled on the request's site.

enabled.label: Enable the /cms/login gate
enabled.help: Set the IP whitelist FIRST: enabling the gate with an empty whitelist locks everyone (including platform administrators) out of /cms/login as soon as enforcement applies.

trustForwardedFor.label: Trust X-Forwarded-For for the client IP
trustForwardedFor.help: When on (default), the whitelist is matched against the first X-Forwarded-For entry. Turn OFF when Jahia is NOT behind a reverse proxy that overwrites that header - otherwise the value is client-spoofable and could be forged to match a whitelisted IP. Off uses the spoof-proof socket address.

whitelist.label: IP whitelist
whitelist.help: Comma-separated IPv4/IPv6 addresses or CIDR blocks allowed through the gate. The client IP is the first X-Forwarded-For entry when present - only enable the gate behind a reverse proxy that overwrites that header.

urls.title: Login / logout routing (global defaults)
urls.help: The pages unauthenticated users are redirected to. Per-site values (each factor's administration page) take precedence. Unlike per-site values, these operator-level URLs may be absolute (e.g. an external SSO portal).

loginUrl.label: Login page URL
loginUrl.help: The page that renders the MFA login UI (the totpui:authentication component). Leave empty to keep Jahia's default login handling.

logoutUrl.label: Logout page URL
logoutUrl.help: Optional custom sign-out page. Leave empty to use Jahia's default logout handling.

reset.title: Locked-out reset requests
reset.help: When a user proves their password but cannot pass any second factor (lost authenticator, no backup codes), the login page offers a "request a reset" action that emails the administrator(s) below. An admin then resets that user's factor(s). Leave empty to disable the action.

resetNotifyEmail.label: Reset-request notification email(s)
resetNotifyEmail.help: Comma-separated administrator address(es) notified of reset requests. Empty = the request action is effectively off (it shows a generic confirmation but notifies no one).

errors.unknownFactor: One of the enforced factors is not an installed factor type.
errors.invalidGraceDays: The grace period must be between 0 and 365 days.
errors.invalidWhitelist: The IP whitelist contains an invalid entry - use IPv4/IPv6 addresses or CIDR blocks.
errors.invalidEmail: The notification email list contains an invalid address.
errors.permissionDenied: You do not have permission to change this configuration.
errors.generic: The configuration could not be saved. Please check the server logs.
